title: context.rs ContextInjector: hook/user content concatenated unescaped into system-reminder — prompt injection
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Security and Access / Perimeter Defense. Location: src/context.rs:10-12 (wrap_system_reminder), 22-48 (inject), 129-137 (inject_all). Violation: wrap_system_reminder simply interpolates content between literal tags with format!. If hook output or rules output contains the literal string '</system-reminder>' or a fake '<system-reminder>' with attacker-crafted content, it breaks out of the reminder envelope and injects arbitrary instructions into the model's prompt. Hook output comes from external processes per hooks.rs — if any hook reads from a web source, MCP tool output, or file whose content is user-controlled, a malicious hook payload can override Claudia's system prompt. The 'test_hook_content_does_not_duplicate_base_sections' test in prompt.rs acknowledges this is a known surface but does not defend against it. Mandated Refactor: In wrap_system_reminder, escape or reject content containing '</system-reminder>', '<system-reminder>', and standalone '</system>' tags. Better: hash-sign the reminder frame with a per-session nonce so injected fakes are distinguishable, e.g. <system-reminder id='abc123'>...</system-reminder abc123>. Alternatively, apply the same treatment as apply_prompt_modification: treat hook-injected text as data, never as instructions, by wrapping in a <user-data> tag that is documented in the system prompt as non-instructional.
